g.data-narration: This diagram illustrates the key concepts of the evolution of blockchain technology.
Understanding 1991: Stuart Haber & W. Scott Stornetta propose a cryptographically secured chain of blocks as the foundational component of this concept.
g.text: 1991: Stuart Haber & W. Scott Stornetta propose a cryptographically secured chain of blocks

g.data-narration: The next step involves 1998: Nick Szabo conceptualizes 'Bit Gold', a decentralized digital currency, which builds upon the previous components.
g.text: 1998: Nick Szabo conceptualizes 'Bit Gold', a decentralized digital currency

g.data-narration: The next step involves 2008: Satoshi Nakamoto publishes the Bitcoin Whitepaper, which builds upon the previous components.
g.text: 2008: Satoshi Nakamoto publishes the Bitcoin Whitepaper

g.data-narration: The next step involves 2009: Genesis Block of Bitcoin is mined, launching the first blockchain, which builds upon the previous components.
g.text: 2009: Genesis Block of Bitcoin is mined, launching the first blockchain

g.data-narration: The next step involves 2013: Vitalik Buterin proposes Ethereum, introducing smart contracts, which builds upon the previous components.
g.text: 2013: Vitalik Buterin proposes Ethereum, introducing smart contracts

g.data-narration: Finally, 2015: Launch of the Ethereum network completes the overall process and delivers the result.
